name: Computer Science
description: Core concepts of computing, from how computers work to algorithms and data structures

concepts.name: What Is Computing?
concepts.description: Computers as machines that follow instructions. Input, processing, output, and storage.

concepts.name: Binary & Data Representation
concepts.description: How computers represent numbers, text, and images using only 0s and 1s.

concepts.name: What Is an Algorithm?
concepts.description: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem. Algorithms exist in cooking, navigation, and code.

concepts.name: Variables & Data Types
concepts.description: Storing values in named containers. Integers, strings, booleans, and why types matter.

concepts.name: Conditionals (If/Else)
concepts.description: Making decisions in code. Boolean expressions, if/else chains, and nested conditions.

concepts.name: Loops
concepts.description: Repeating actions with for loops and while loops. Loop counters, conditions, and avoiding infinite loops.

concepts.name: Functions
concepts.description: Packaging reusable logic into named blocks. Parameters, return values, and scope.

concepts.name: Arrays & Lists
concepts.description: Storing ordered collections of values. Indexing, iteration, and common operations.

concepts.name: Debugging
concepts.description: Finding and fixing errors. Reading error messages, using print statements, and systematic elimination.

concepts.name: Sorting Algorithms
concepts.description: Bubble sort, selection sort, and merge sort. Comparing efficiency and understanding O(n log n).

concepts.name: Dictionaries & Hash Maps
concepts.description: Key-value storage. When to use a dictionary vs a list, and how hashing works conceptually.

concepts.name: Recursion
concepts.description: Functions that call themselves. Base cases, recursive cases, and the call stack.

concepts.name: Big-O Notation
concepts.description: Describing algorithm efficiency. O(1), O(n), O(n²), O(log n) and what they mean in practice.

concepts.name: Trees & Graphs
concepts.description: Hierarchical and networked data structures. Binary trees, traversals, and graph basics.
